title: What an American thinks about Leichtenstein

transcript.text: What an american thinks about leichtenstein leichtenstein is like the smallest country
In the world or I guess like the fourth smallest country in the world after the vatican san marino
And monaco it's smaller than um light than luxembourg and I only was there for like a
Couple of hours I rode in there from the hills from um switzerland and then I rode across
And um I went across some big big bridge and maybe that was the bridge that took me into leichtenstein
But uh there are these gypsy ladies they asked me a question they stopped me on the bridge while I'm
Riding my bike down it they asked me some question in some language and I was like I don't know I'm
An american and they were like ah and they like stormed off but um then I go in there and yeah
They're in leichtenstein they use they use uh the swiss money the swiss franc or whatever and um
Um um they there were no real towns it was just like some buildings around
And uh next to this highway I should have ridden gone out of my way and ridden to their capital city
The city of leichtenstein and checked it out but it was up this valley up a dead end valley so you
Would have had to come back the same way you go and I didn't really want to do that probably should
Have but uh but um yeah I only really have that one I just passed through I probably should have
Stayed longer but it's basically like switzerland just with different uh yeah really small really
Small and like one tiny town which is a capital that I didn't go to but uh yeah it's basically just
Part of switzerland and there you go
